fact_texts: User had an opinion about being an adopted son: User would hate to be an adopted son.
User knocked a lantern from a man's head during a procession, was threatened with death, fled barefoot to Dojima, ran faster than ever, and avoided drawing a sword.
User says "This is my own money" and believes buying things with their own money is fine.
User spoke to student Tezuka, asked him to stop going to the Shinchi quarter, promised help, and they made an agreement that Tezuka would sacrifice his hair if he returned to Shinchi.
User teased student Yamada about his superstition of avoiding the syllable 'shi' while discussing upcoming plays.
User was unconcerned with the way of society and had an inborn nature to act always in their own way.
User believes their taste in sword fitting comes from early work, though their skill was never more than that of an amateur.
User disappeared into a closet when guests arrived, disliked them, took supper early, drank wine, and hid in the closet until guests left.
User hated having a towel on their face and never wore one.
User made light of the mock gentility of neighbors with boyish pride and conceit.
User had an opinion about being an adopted son: User would hate to be an adopted son.

User's brother had many friends who came in the evening to discuss questions of the day.

User's brother's conversation topics included Rekko of Mito and Shungaku of Echizen.

Yukichi entered Ogata's home-school in Nagasaki, studied under many teachers—including interpreter Narahayashi, doctor Narahayashi, and affluent physician Ishikawa Osho, who refused to teach him—and later began systematic Dutch study in Osaka, progressing quickly to become one of the best students; he obtained a permit to leave home to study gunnery under Master Ogata of Osaka by rewriting the petition to state studying gunnery.
User will write a letter for the father, who is a practicing physician, and the father will have no difficulty finding him at his house in Nihombashi, Himonocho in Yedo.

User hated having a towel on their face and never wore one.

User forged a letter to Sembaya, using Kuroganeya's name, to secure lodging and a boat fare to Osaka, and the proprietor accepted it, allowing the user to travel despite lacking money for food on board.

After his brother died on September third, Yukichi returned home by Inland Sea, became head of the Fukuzawa family branch, and was required to observe fifty days of mourning.
User had an opinion about being an adopted son: User would hate to be an adopted son.

Yukichi was named after the second syllable of his father's book title Jo Yu Jo Rei, a collection of Ming dynasty Chinese law books, and his father owned over fifteen hundred volumes including rare Chinese law books titled Shangyi Tiao-li, acquired on December 12 in the fifth year of Temp5.
User was unconcerned with the way of society and had an inborn nature to act always in their own way.

User and companions engaged in friendly street fights in Osaka's busiest areas, moving locations daily between Dotombori and Junkei-machi, with Numata Umpei from Shinshu being particularly good at fighting.
User disappeared into a closet when guests arrived, disliked them, took supper early, drank wine, and hid in the closet until guests left.
User endured cold until spring at age fifteen or sixteen.

Yukichi entered Ogata's home-school in Nagasaki, studied under many teachers—including interpreter Narahayashi, doctor Narahayashi, and affluent physician Ishikawa Osho, who refused to teach him—and later began systematic Dutch study in Osaka, progressing quickly to become one of the best students; he obtained a permit to leave home to study gunnery under Master Ogata of Osaka by rewriting the petition to state studying gunnery.

A small kakemono with Rai Sanyo’s calligraphy was sold for two hu; Taigado’s painting of a man under a willow tree sold for two ryo and two hu; other calligraphy by Sorai and Togai brought little sum.

Yukichi and his family sold all household items, including a large collection of over fifteen hundred books, paintings, swords, trays, cups, and drinking vessels, to pay off debts entirely and reach the required forty ryo.
